steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The text claims that ‘steady energy loads’ are an ‘absolute prerequisite’ for wireless communication devices to be powered piezoelectrically. The correct choice must directly support that claim — meaning it should show that wireless devices fail or falter without steady power.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "The near-constant kinetic vibration of a spacecraft makes it possible to power its wireless communication devices using only non-CFRP piezoelectric harvesters.": This says non-CFRP harvesters are sufficient, which contradicts the passage’s focus on the CFRP design as the key advance. It doesn’t address the need for steady loads at all. ✗
  • "Intermittent or unpredictable electrical supply undermines the efficacy of wireless communication devices.": If unsteady power undermines wireless devices, then steady power is indeed a prerequisite — exactly what the text claims. This directly validates the requirement. ✓
  • "The CFRP electrode is incompatible with most wireless communication devices.": This would undermine the passage’s conclusion, not support it. ✗
  • "The high conductivity of the CFRP is what makes the energy output from a piezoelectric harvester sufficient for wireless communication devices.": This explains a mechanism behind why CFRP works, but it doesn’t show that steady loads are a necessity — it doesn’t address what happens without them. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
